title: HTTP request redirections should not be open to forging attacks
description: Open redirection occurs when an application uses user-controllable data to redirect users to a URL. An attacker with malicious intent could manipulate a user to browse into a specially crafted URL, such as https://trusted.example.com?url=evil.example.com, to redirect the victim to his evil domain. Tricking users into sending the malicious HTTP request is usually the main task of exploiting an open redirection.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detect Redirect() or Response.Redirect() calls with parameters that are NOT hardcoded strings and NOT validated by IsLocalUrl or IsWellFormedUriString
logic.triggering_conditions: (Method call is Redirect or Response.Redirect) AND (Argument is variable/parameter) AND (NOT validated) AND (NOT in excluded contexts)
logic.reasoning: This rule detects open redirect vulnerabilities where Redirect() or Response.Redirect() is called with user-controlled parameters without validation. Pattern matches Redirect calls with variable arguments (not string literals). Excludes hardcoded strings ("/home", "https://..."), safe redirect methods (RedirectToAction), and cases where validation is present in the context (IsLocalUrl, IsWellFormedUriString). AST-first approach targeting CallExpression nodes.
